     The Numbers Business Report

The Peanut Butter Falcon (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $20,457,158Details
International Box Office $2,734,393
Worldwide Box Office $23,191,551
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,722,730
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,282,393
Total $4,005,123
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Zak is a young man with Down syndrome who runs away from a residential nursing home to follow his dream of attending the professional wrestling school of his idol, The Salt Water Redneck. A strange turn of events pairs him on the road with Tyler, a small time outlaw on the run, who becomes Zak’s unlikely coach and ally. Together they wind through deltas, elude capture, drink whisky, find God, catch fish, and convince Eleanor, a kind nursing home employee charged with Zak’s return, to join them on their journey.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$204,793 (1.0% of total gross)
Legs:6.78 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:88.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$6,200,000 (worldwide box office is 3.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:17 opening theaters/1,490 max. theaters, 5.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $26,710,435

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 9th, 2019 (Limited) by Roadside Attractions
August 23rd, 2019 (Expands Wide) by Roadside Attractions
International Releases: August 30th, 2019 (Wide), released as O Falcão Manteiga de Amendoim (Portugal)
October 18th, 2019 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
October 24th, 2019 (Wide), released as Арахисовый сокол (Russia (CIS))
November 15th, 2019 (Wide) (New Zealand)
December 20th, 2019 (Wide) (Netherlands)
Video Release: November 5th, 2019 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic content, language throughout, some violence and smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2579 (Cert #52082), 5/22/2019)
Running Time: 93 minutes
Plot point: Coming of Age, Down Syndrome, Runaway, Wrestling
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Armory Films, Bona Fide, Roadside Attractions, Endeavor Content
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Zak is a young man with Down syndrome who runs away from a residential nursing home to follow his dream of attending the professional wrestling school of his idol, The Salt Water Redneck. A strange turn of events pairs him on the road with Tyler, a small time outlaw on the run, who becomes Zak’s unlikely coach and ally. Together they wind through deltas, elude capture, drink whisky, find God, catch fish, and convince Eleanor, a kind nursing home employee charged with Zak’s return, to join them on their journey.
